{{Quote|At approximately 3900 BBY, Queen Tasia sponsored one of the last of her world's colonization missions. The three ships—''Beneficient Tasia'', ''Constant'', and ''Mother Vima''—departed Grizmallt and vanished from sight.|A Galactic Alliance historical document|<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>}}
'''''Beneficent Tasia''''' was a [[Colony ship/Legends|colony ship]] that departed the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Grizmallt/Legends|Grizmallt]]<ref name="GB" /> in approximately [[3900 BBY/Legends|3900 BBY]]<ref name="NEC" /> in an expedition to seek fortune and glory. ''Beneficent Tasia'' was accompanied by its support [[Starship/Legends|ships]], ''[[Constant]]'' and ''[[Mother Vima]]''.<ref name="GB" /> The ships were filled with [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Grizmallti/Legends|Grizmallti]] [[Refugee/Legends|refugees]] fleeing a [[Grizmallti civil war/Legends|civil war]] on Grizmallt,<ref name="Nexus">''[[Nexus of Power]]''</ref> and they were sponsored by Grizmallt's [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Elsinoré den Tasia]] and blessed by a famous [[Unidentified famous Jedi Master|Jedi Master]].<ref name="GB" />

The expedition, led by the [[Scout/Legends|explorer]] [[Kwilaan/Legends|Kwilaan]],<ref name="Secrets">''[[Secrets of Naboo]]''</ref> targeted the dangerous southern quadrant of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and shortly after the depature, Grizmallt lost contact with the ''Beneficient Tasia'' and assumed the expedition had failed. Although the ships were severely damaged and unable to return contact, the expedition arrived on the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]],<ref name="GB" /> although one of the ships crashed.<ref name="Crisis">{{SW|url=explore/the-clone-wars/ep418|text="Crisis on Naboo" - ''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide|nolive=1}}"</ref> Some [[Scholar/Legends|scholars]] claimed that a contingent of the colonists landed on Naboo's [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Rori/Legends|Rori]] first.<ref name="SWG">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided Quick Reference Guide]]''</ref>

The passengers of the ships,<ref name="GB" /> which included a member of [[House Korden]],<ref name="Intrigue">''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]''</ref> [[Colonist/Legends|colonized]] the world, becoming known as the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] and developing a thriving society<ref name="GB" /> after [[Human-Gungan conflicts/Legends|clashing]] with the native [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]].<ref name="NEC" /> In [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],<ref name="Legends-Date">{{TCWdates|418}}</ref> a holographic representation of the crashed starship appeared as part of that [[Standard year/Legends|year's]] annual [[Festival of Light/Legends|Festival of Light]], which commemorated the 847th anniverary of Naboo joining the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="Crisis episode">{{TCW|Crisis on Naboo}}</ref>

The ''Beneficient Tasia'' was mentioned in a [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|historical document]] published by the [[Historical Council]] in [[36 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Beneficient Tasia'' was first mentioned in the [[Royal House of Naboo/Legends|Royal Naboo]] DataBank entry in the [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''.<ref name="GB" /> The [[StarWars.com]] episode guide for "[[Crisis on Naboo]]," the eighteenth episode of the [[The Clone Wars: Season Four|fourth season]] of ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'',<ref name="Crisis" /> which aired on [[February 10]], [[2012]],<ref name="Ep guide">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Episode Guide]]''</ref> stated that a Grizmallt colony ship crashed on Naboo; however, it was not specified which vessel crashed.<ref name="Crisis" />
